page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. This instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and includes a 133 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. It does not contain local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 7387.52 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 210.01 GB/sec. Multi-core processing is highly efficient, as evidenced by top-tier scores in Geekbench multi-core, software compilation with Clang, and Passmark CPU physics tests. Additionally, the instance demonstrates strong performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput. However, single-core stress-ng and text processing benchmarks fall into the average tier, and text generation for small LLM models is relatively poor.

Given its ARM64 architecture and high core density, the c8gb.16xlarge offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is supported by the high-speed 133 Gbps network interface. This server is highly suited for software compilation, database operations, high-throughput static web serving, and multi-threaded data compression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the 64-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this ARM64 instance provides 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM and a 133 Gbps baseline network interface. It features no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads at 466.21 GB/sec, alongside exceptional performance in multi-core software compilation, database operations, and static web serving throughput. While single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation are more moderate, the instance delivers high resource density and parallel processing efficiency. It is highly suited for multi-threaded workloads, software development pipelines, high-throughput web serving, and database management.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.16xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 133 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-throughput parallel workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance delivering high-density multi-core performance and top-tier memory bandwidth for parallel workloads and high-throughput web serving.

Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM and a 133 Gbps baseline network interface
